109566.explanation: No rule, but nice to strive for a proportional distribution.

109567.explanation: By exception and for very important matters.

109568.explanation: If good and clear agreements can be made.

109570.explanation: Tailored approach. Not everything can always be allowed, but a positive attitude is preferred.

109573.explanation: The employer must ensure adequate housing but not necessarily on their own business premises.

109574.explanation: Not necessarily; sometimes thinning out is necessary. It depends on the situation.

109577.explanation: Provided this can be organized jointly with local associations and businesses.

109579.explanation: Not only the noise standards but also the time frame within which noise is permitted.

109582.explanation: Under conditions and with a maximum amount.

109585.explanation: Encourage and facilitate. In Baarle there is a music academy where lesson costs are low. This music academy is subsidized by the municipalities of Baarle-Nassau and Baarle-Hertog.

109586.explanation: Sports clubs receive subsidies. Beyond that, sports clubs should be financially self-sufficient.

109588.explanation: Recently, extra funds were already allocated for this in the municipal budget. The current staffing level is at the desired strength.

109589.explanation: At this moment, there is no need for this.

109590.explanation: We see this as a regional facility, not a local one.

109593.explanation: In parks with a tourist-recreational perspective, this is not desirable.

109596.explanation: Exemption possibilities are already arranged for some municipal taxes. There is no need to extend this.

109598.explanation: We should remain critical whether the deployment of youth workers is sufficiently effective.

109599.explanation: Baarle-Nassau has actually already started this. Citizen participation is used to determine the locations of charging stations.

109602.explanation: Both quality and price serve as selection criteria.

109603.explanation: Provided this is budgetarily possible. Given the cuts in the municipal fund and the discontinuation of the extra enclave allowance, this seems difficult to realize.

109605.explanation: We do not support further increases. The tourist tax has already been raised to an average applicable rate in the current council term.

109606.explanation: There must be room for expansion of local SMEs and also establishment opportunities for new companies.

109608.explanation: Certainly not unlimited. However, assigning a specific quota to status holders is considered inevitable to meet the targets imposed by higher authorities.

109611.explanation: We consider renewal inevitable but the municipality must continue to facilitate a sports park in Ulicoten.

109615.explanation: At this moment, the supply of social rental housing in Baarle-Nassau is relatively low and 30% as a share is the appropriate target to strive for. In the long term, the local need should be considered rather than a predetermined percentage.

109620.explanation: According to BAARLE!, environmental impact is decisive. We are also not in favor of large-scale intensive farming.

109621.explanation: Cooperation is fine and also necessary. However, we value the local autonomy of our municipality.

109566.explanation: According to the CDA, the right people must be on the executive board. Gender should not be a decisive factor.

109568.explanation: The current CDA alderman Adams has even already taken initiatives for this.

109570.explanation: By only naming a shop or campsite, you limit other ambitions of the agricultural sector. Other options include generating green gas or growing raw materials for bio-based construction materials, for example.

109574.explanation: Good agreements must be made with initiators. Afterwards, these must be properly observed.

109579.explanation: As a municipality, we should not impose stricter standards.

109582.explanation: This must be assessed per application.

109590.explanation: When there is a clear need, this can be considered.

109598.explanation: The effect must be measured: is the deployment of youth work effective or not?

109599.explanation: Look at what the demand is.

109602.explanation: The municipality must primarily keep costs manageable.

109606.explanation: Applications should be thoroughly reviewed together with the initiator.

109611.explanation: Not necessarily renewing, but definitely providing a good future perspective for the residents of Ulicoten and its associations.

109621.explanation: We certainly need to collaborate MORE with BAARLE-HERTOG.

109566.explanation: In principle, we support an equal distribution of men and women in the executive board, although this can be difficult in practice with our small board. We mainly find it important to have capable administrators.

109567.explanation: Referendums are often black-and-white; you are either for or against. For specific topics, we see more promise in a citizens' council, a promising form of participation.

109568.explanation: To save costs, it may be possible that citizens help maintain green spaces in the long term, but the responsibility must remain with the municipality.

109570.explanation: Whether another or additional purpose for an agricultural business is possible depends on the location, the environmental plan, the type of business, odor nuisance, etc.

109573.explanation: If employers want to help their seasonal workers find housing, that is fine, but housing should not be tied to employment.

109574.explanation: The VPB believes that for every tree cut down at least one, preferably two native trees should be planted. For example, in a new Baols Burger Forest.

109577.explanation: We are in favor of a ban on consumer fireworks because the costs to society are too high. Instead, we want a professional, communal fireworks show.

109579.explanation: The standards are strict enough; they just need to be better enforced.

109582.explanation: We support people who struggle to make ends meet through the Participation Scheme.

109585.explanation: This option already exists at the Academy for Speech and Music, which we subsidize per student and per resident.

109586.explanation: We must allocate our scarce resources to associations with a strong foundation and a healthy future perspective.

109588.explanation: We see no urgent reason to deploy more enforcement officers (BOAs). Moreover, due to budget cuts from the national government, we do not have the funds for that. Police tasks should be carried out by the police.

109589.explanation: Camera surveillance should be used selectively for structural problems. That is not the case here.

109590.explanation: If there is a need, we have no objection, but we wonder if we are the best place for this as a border municipality.

109593.explanation: It depends on the location and conditions. We also believe that any necessary investments must be made by the holiday park itself. No taxpayer money should be used for this.

109595.explanation: The municipality must support people on social assistance in their search for paid work.

109596.explanation: In practice, this rarely happens, but the possibility of debt forgiveness should remain.

109598.explanation: We would prefer to see an increase in the number of hours the youth worker is active in our municipality.

109599.explanation: Additional charging points for electric cars are regularly installed and this must grow along with the number of electric cars.

109602.explanation: Quality youth care can actually prevent other, additional care needs.

109603.explanation: There is no room to lower the property tax unless we drastically cut social services, and we do not want that.

109605.explanation: The tourist tax has already increased in recent years to balance the budget.

109606.explanation: We believe it is currently unnecessary to develop more industrial sites.

109608.explanation: Legally, we must give priority to status holders, and there are understandable reasons for this. At the same time, we understand that this can be frustrating for people who were born and raised here.

109611.explanation: We want sport park De Bremer to be renewed in a way that aligns with local needs, the future prospects of associations and the community, and the capabilities of our municipality.

109620.explanation: Due to national and provincial laws and regulations, options are limited, but we support reducing the livestock population.

109621.explanation: Collaboration within the ABG framework is necessary. Independently, we would not be able to perform all tasks.

109566.explanation: Fractie Ulicoten wants a substantive and politically sensitive executive board capable of effectively implementing policy. We do not care whether board members are male or female.

109567.explanation: Residents should have the ability to make adjustments without having to wait for an election.

109568.explanation: Provided it improves quality, people want to do it, and good conditions are in place.

109570.explanation: In principle yes, but we must prevent an oversupply.

109573.explanation: We gladly leave that to the employers themselves.

109574.explanation: Basically, this is a good principle. However, it does not apply to trees in private gardens. This exception must be clearly defined.

109577.explanation: Of course, we want to cooperate with other organizations that wish to do this.

109579.explanation: The existing policy suffices; the government does not need to create new policy for this.

109582.explanation: Residents with an income up to 130% of the statutory social minimum can already make use of the Participation Scheme, which allows them to apply for €200 per person per year to participate in sports, music, or other activities.

109585.explanation: This is provided through the subsidy the municipality of Baarle-Nassau grants to the music associations in our municipality and the music academy in Baarle.

109586.explanation: We consider all associations to be of great value to the community.

109588.explanation: Enforcement must be increased and improved.

109589.explanation: In places where we know that unacceptable activities take place, we believe the mayor should invoke article 151c of the Municipalities Act. For this, the municipal council must first establish a regulation.

109590.explanation: Cooperation and good coordination in the region are necessary. Having all facilities within one’s own municipality does not seem feasible.

109593.explanation: Provided there is no longer a touristic perspective and strict conditions are met. Then this could be a possible measure to temporarily relieve pressure on the housing market.

109595.explanation: ‘Must’ is a strong word, but we do want to actively encourage this.

109596.explanation: We consider this a good measure to ensure that those who would otherwise really get into trouble have a safety net.

109598.explanation: We specifically want to invest more in youth work.

109599.explanation: We want to work on increasing the coverage of charging stations. At the same time, there must always remain sufficient parking space for cars with combustion engines.

109602.explanation: Care must be good and organizations must have their operations in order, so that we can verify whether care is provided effectively and efficiently.

109603.explanation: Given the current financial position, that would mean (additional) cuts to the level of facilities, and we do not want that. The property tax (ozb) is currently around the national average.

109605.explanation: Parallel to expanding the tourist offer, the tourist tax may increase provided the competitive position remains intact.

109606.explanation: We have a positive attitude towards the expansion of the Baarle-East industrial estate. We do not consider new business parks necessary.

109608.explanation: Our residents have just as much right to housing as refugees with a residence permit.

109611.explanation: Preserving all current facilities for tennis, football, events, and the senior citizens’ association.

109615.explanation: The housing stock in Baarle-Nassau consists of about 11% social rental housing, while there are still hundreds of people from our municipality on the waiting list.

109620.explanation: The national government and the province already impose rules on our farmers. We will not add additional layers to those.

109621.explanation: We do not consider the ABG collaboration and the ABG organization in its current form sustainable.

statements.title: The municipality of Baarle-Nassau must reduce its cooperation with Alphen-Chaam and Gilze en Rijen (the ABG organization).
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on public safety must be deployed in Baarle-Nassau.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
More CCTV surveillance must be installed in Baarle-Nassau.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The residents of Baarle-Nassau must be able to give their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Baarle-Nassau moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on youth work.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
People and organizations that help young people in the municipality with various things.

statements.title: At least 30% of the newly built homes in Baarle-Nassau must be social housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Baarle-Nassau moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: A women's shelter for victims of domestic violence must be established in Baarle-Nassau.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Sportpark De Bremer in Baarle-Nassau must be renovated.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
More business parks must be developed in Baarle-Nassau.
The municipality must enact stricter rules to reduce the number of animals on farms.
